name: quantum-computing-for-everyone
description: Chris Bernhardt's MIT Press introduction to quantum computing — qubits, superposition, entanglement, Bell's inequality, quantum gates, Shor's algorithm, Grover's algorithm, and the real-world impact.
useCase: Understanding qubits and superposition, quantum gates and circuits, entanglement and Bell's inequality, quantum algorithms (Shor, Grover), quantum cryptography (BB84), and the impact of quantum computing.
knownRisks: The book uses linear algebra at a level that may still challenge readers without any math background
Quantum computing is evolving rapidly — some specifics about machine capabilities may be dated
Not all quantum computing claims made by companies have been validated in peer-reviewed research
The book covers theory (software), not hardware implementation details
